title: Roldana pinetorum
intro: Roldana pinetorum, also known as Pine Roldana, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Roldana pinetorum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats, and as a topical application to treat skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Roldana pinetorum is classified within the family Asteraceae. This family is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a group of flowering plants that includes over 23,000 species. Roldana pinetorum is further classified within the genus Roldana, which includes over 30 species of flowering plants. The genus Roldana is native to the mountainous regions of Central America.
uses: Roldana pinetorum is not widely documented in terms of its medicinal uses. However, its plant family, Asteraceae, has members that are used in traditional medicine for various purposes, such as treating fever and inflammation. benefits: Roldana pinetorum, a lesser-known plant species, is reported to have potential health benefits due to its unique chemical composition.
